prompt: Give me a short pep talk before I start a hard task I have been putting off all morning.
response:
You've got this! Starting a challenging task can be intimidating, but remember that taking the first step is always the hardest part. Once you begin, you'll build momentum and it will become easier as you progress. Here's a quick reminder of your capabilities:

- You possess the skills and knowledge required to tackle this task. Believe in your expertise and trust your abilities.

- Breaking down the task into smaller, manageable chunks can make it less daunting. Focus on one step at a time and celebrate the small wins along the way.

- Hard work and dedication always pay off. Embrace the challenge as an opportunity for growth and learning. You'll come out stronger and more accomplished on the other side.

- Procrastination only delays the inevitable, and the task will not complete itself. Starting early can prevent last-minute stress and allow you to produce your best work.

- You've overcome difficult situations before, and this is no different. Summon that resilience and determination to power through!

Take a deep breath, stay focused, and dive into it. You will feel an incredible sense of accomplishment once it's done. Keep up the motivation and go crush it!
